summary: PR #2331 matched ftCo_FlyReflect by marking the object Matching, adjusting ftCo_FlyReflect.c code shape around fly-reflect wall/ceiling collision helpers, replacing sqrtf with a local fake_sqrtf using __frsqrte, adding stack/local padding and a narrow dont_inline pragma, and renaming several ftCo_FlyReflect .sdata2 symbols to local @ labels. Bot output reported 4 new matches including ftCo_800C17CC, ftCo_800C18A8, ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll, and .sdata2.

key_files.role: Primary decomp source updated for ftCo_FlyReflect matching; changed includes, stack padding locals, helper inlining shape, fake_sqrtf, control-flow expansion, and dont_inline scoping.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_800C17CC, ftCo_800C18A8, ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll as new 100% matches

key_files.role: Build configuration changed ftCo_FlyReflect.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Adjusted .sdata2 symbol names for ftCo_FlyReflect literals from address-style ftCo_804D8C40..58 labels to @221/@259/@260/@261/@262.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt .sdata2 entries at 0x804D8C40 through 0x804D8C58 renamed
text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_FlyReflect .sdata2 as new 100% match

what_changed.text: Moved ftCo_FlyReflect.c to Matching in configure.py.

Reworked ftCo_800C15F4 and ftCo_800C1718 local padding from byte arrays to u32 pad locals; ftCo_800C1718 was wrapped in #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop and used gobj->user_data instead of GET_FIGHTER.
Added ftCo_800C1718_inline with duplicated ceiling-reflect logic and used it in ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll where RETURN_IF(ftCo_800C1718(gobj)) had been used.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added ftCo_800C1718_inline and ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll hunk

what_changed.text: Expanded ftCo_800C17CC from two helper calls into explicit collision/reflection logic with PAD_STACK(0x10), local declarations, and explicit ret handling.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author mentions uncertainty about stack pad at beginning of the function

what_changed.text: Added fake_sqrtf using __frsqrte Newton-Raphson refinement and volatile float storage; ftCo_800C18A8 now calls fake_sqrtf instead of sqrtf.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fake_sqrtf addition and ftCo_800C18A8 sqrtf replacement

what_changed.text: Expanded ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll helper-return logic into an explicit tmp variable and nested calls to ftCo_800C15F4 / ftCo_800C1718.
Renamed five .sdata2 symbols at 0x804D8C40, 0x804D8C44, 0x804D8C48, 0x804D8C50, and 0x804D8C58 to @ local labels.

smart_moves.text: The PR kept the object-level promotion explicit by changing configure.py only after the source and symbols changes produced bot-reported new matches.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report with 4 new matches

smart_moves.text: The author called out uncertainty about the stack pad approach in the PR body instead of presenting the tactic as a settled convention.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author says they were unsure how to do the stack pad without creating rodata

smart_moves.text: The dont_inline pragma was narrowly scoped with push/pop around ftCo_800C1718 rather than left as a broad file-level setting.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on before ftCo_800C1718, #pragma pop after it

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matching change needs stack padding or local lifetime steering, preserve the uncertainty and verifier evidence; this PR used PAD_STACK, explicit pad locals, and local declaration reshaping to close small remaining gaps.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author notes uncertainty about stack pad
raw_diff: PAD_STACK(0x10), u32 pad locals, local declaration reshaping
text_corpus: bot report shows ftCo_800C17CC, ftCo_800C18A8, and ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll reached 100%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small percent gaps in a nearly matched function can be due to helper call shape, inlining, local declaration order, or math intrinsic expansion rather than high-level behavior changes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ftCo_800C1718_inline addition and callsite change
text_corpus: bot report lists +1 byte improvements to three functions reaching 100%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Literal/sdata2 metadata may be adjusted alongside a code match when the report shows the same translation unit's data section reaching 100%, but the PR evidence does not explain the data-ownership rationale.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt renamed five ftCo_804D8Cxx labels to @ labels
text_corpus: bot report lists ftCo_FlyReflect .sdata2 as 58.33% to 100.00%

naming_conventions.text: The PR changed anonymous/local .sdata2 float and double labels from ftCo_804D8C40-style address names to compiler-like @221/@259/@260/@261/@262 labels.
Local temporary names used for matching include pad, pad1..pad4, normal, ret, and tmp; evidence supports these as local code-shape names, not as broader naming standards.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use explicit stack padding via PAD_STACK and zero-initialized u32 pad locals when byte-array padding creates unwanted rodata or wrong code shape.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: stack pad uncertainty without creating rodata
raw_diff: u8 arrays changed to u32 pad locals; PAD_STACK(0x10)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Existing standards require explicit verifier evidence for PAD_STACK and padding tactics; the bot report supports final matching, but the PR slice does not include objdiff details or reviewer discussion validating this as preferred style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scope #pragma dont_inline tightly with push/pop around a single helper when call/inlining shape matters.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on around ftCo_800C1718 / #pragma pop
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Pragmas are exceptions under loaded standards; this PR evidence shows narrow scope but not adjacent-function validation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Create a separate inline version of a helper to satisfy one callsite while preserving a non-inlined function body elsewhere.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added ftCo_800C1718_inline and changed one RETURN_IF call to use it
raw_diff: ftCo_800C17CC still calls/duplicates ftCo_800C1718 behavior separately
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: Potentially generated-looking duplication; should be reviewed against authored-source style and verifier output before treating as a reusable pattern.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace standard sqrtf with a local fake_sqrtf using __frsqrte refinement and volatile float storage to match expected PPC codegen.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: bot report says ftCo_800C18A8 reached 100%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: The helper name itself indicates a match tactic; do not generalize without checking local math idioms and objdiff evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Expand RETURN_IF/helper-call control flow into explicit tmp/ret variables when boolean return code shape needs exact branching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_note: This may reduce readability and should not outrank normal control-flow forms unless verifier evidence requires it.

review_feedback.text: Author stated this was their first match PR and asked for guidance, specifically noting uncertainty about stack padding without creating rodata.
review_feedback.author: jellejurre

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev bot reported matched code +1268 bytes, linked code +1860 bytes, matched data +32 bytes, linked data +32 bytes, and 4 new matches for ftCo_FlyReflect.
review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]

review_feedback.text: No human review comments or review records are present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: stack pad without creating rodata PAD_STACK

Weak or missing evidence for reviewer intent because no human reviews or review comments are loaded.
The PR body admits uncertainty about stack padding, so padding/pragmas/fake_sqrtf should be treated as tactics used in this PR, not accepted global style.
The bot report supports new matches but does not provide detailed objdiff, adjacent-function, or build logs in the loaded slice.
The symbols.txt rename rationale is not explained beyond the diff and bot-reported .sdata2 improvement.

accepted_candidate_records.title: ftCo_FlyReflect match used stack padding, inline-shape, and math intrinsic tactics
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2331 matched ftCo_FlyReflect by combining narrow stack/local-shape edits, a scoped dont_inline pragma, an inline helper variant, explicit boolean control flow, fake_sqrtf using __frsqrte, and .sdata2 symbol metadata changes. Bot output reported four new matches, but the PR slice lacks human review and detailed objdiff logs.

source_update_candidates.title: Document that stack padding and pragma/inlining tactics need narrow verifier evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2331 is a useful example of stack padding, scoped dont_inline, local declaration reshaping, and inline-helper duplication used to complete a match, while the author explicitly noted uncertainty. Consider cross-linking this PR as an example under existing matching-tactics and pragma standards, with a warning that the slice lacks human review and detailed objdiff logs.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: author uncertainty about stack pad
raw_diff: PAD_STACK, pad locals, scoped dont_inline
text_corpus: bot report with 4 new matches
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR gives concrete evidence of tactics in use, but should not be promoted as broad accepted style without curator review.

source_update_candidates.title: ftCo_FlyReflect.c became Matching in PR #2331
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2331 changed Object(NonMatching, "melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_FlyReflect.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_FlyReflect.c") in configure.py.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a path-scoped historical fact supported by the diff and merge metadata; curator should decide whether to add it to path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: ftCo_FlyReflect .sdata2 labels changed to @ labels in PR #2331
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2331 renamed five ftCo_FlyReflect .sdata2 symbols at 0x804D8C40 through 0x804D8C58 from ftCo_804D8Cxx labels to @221/@259/@260/@261/@262, and the bot report showed the unit's .sdata2 moving from 58.33% to 100.00%.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact for data-symbol history; rationale is not explained in review, so curator should keep it factual and narrow.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that fake_sqrtf is a general project standard; this PR only shows it used locally to match ftCo_800C18A8.
Do not infer human reviewer approval of padding, pragmas, or .sdata2 renames; no human review comments are present.
Do not treat duplicated ftCo_800C1718 logic as preferred authored style without comparing local source and objdiff evidence.
Do not claim adjacent-function regression checks were run; the loaded slice only includes the bot summary report.
